title: Wpromote OpenCode Configuration
description: Configuration for @wpro-eng/opencode-config — the Wpromote OpenCode plugin that syncs team assets on startup. Place this file at .opencode/wpromote.json (or .jsonc) in your project, or at ~/.config/opencode/wpromote.json globally.

$schema.description: Path or URL to this JSON Schema for editor autocompletion.

disable.description: Asset disable list. Entries can be typed ("skill:example"), untyped ("example"), glob patterns ("skill:deprecated-*"), or negated ("!skill:keep-this"). Later entries override earlier ones.

agentOverrides.description: Partial patch-style overrides applied to team agent configs before injection. Use this to change specific values (for example temperature) without redefining the whole agent. Built-in team agents accept either descriptive or LOTR names as keys (for example `planner` or `treebeard`). Local opencode.json agent entries still take precedence and fully replace the team agent config.

additionalProperties.description: Partial agent config patch for a team agent name.

permission.description: Permission config. Supports full replacement of top-level keys; nested objects merge with team defaults during apply.

installMethod.description: How skills, plugins, and MCPs are installed locally. "link" creates symlinks (default, faster, auto-updates with package). "copy" creates independent copies.

agentNames.description: Agent naming theme. "descriptive" uses role-based names (orchestrator, explorer, etc.). "lotr" uses Tolkien character names (gandalf, legolas, etc.).

dryRun.description: When true, analyzes what would change without writing any files. Useful for previewing sync behavior.

models.description: Override the model used by team agents. Built-in team agents accept either descriptive or LOTR names as keys (for example `orchestrator` or `gandalf`), regardless of the active naming theme. Omitted agents or empty string values fall back to the plugin default.

orchestration.description: Orchestration runtime configuration for delegation, concurrency, fallback, and tmux integration.

providerMode.description: Default provider routing mode for subagent tasks.

vision.description: Enable the vision multimodal analysis tool.

selfHealing.description: Enable self-healing retry policies for failed tasks.

limits.description: Concurrency, retry, and timeout limits for delegated tasks.

maxConcurrent.description: Maximum number of concurrent delegated subagent tasks.

maxRetries.description: Maximum retry attempts per failed task.

retryBackoffMs.description: Delay in milliseconds between retry attempts.

runtimeFallback.description: Provider fallback configuration. When the primary provider fails, the runtime can fall back to the next provider in the chain.

enabled.description: Enable automatic provider fallback on errors.

retryOnErrors.description: HTTP status codes that trigger a fallback attempt.

maxFallbackAttempts.description: Maximum number of fallback attempts before giving up.

cooldownSeconds.description: Cooldown period in seconds between fallback attempts.

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for each fallback attempt.

notifyOnFallback.description: Show a notification when a fallback is triggered.

providerOrder.description: Ordered list of providers to try. First provider is primary; subsequent providers are fallbacks.

hooks.description: Hook configuration for orchestration lifecycle events.

disabled.description: List of hook names to disable.

telemetry.description: Enable telemetry counters for hook invocations.

maxItems.description: Maximum number of notifications to retain.

categories.description: Per-category overrides for provider routing and concurrency limits. Category names are free-form strings (normalized to lowercase at runtime).

routing.description: Map of category name to provider mode. Overrides the top-level providerMode for tasks in that category.

maxConcurrent.description: Map of category name to max concurrent tasks. Overrides the top-level limits.maxConcurrent for that category.

recovery.description: Startup recovery configuration for orphaned tasks from prior sessions.

autoResumeOnStart.description: Automatically recover queued/running/retrying tasks on startup by moving them back to queued state.

tmux.description: Tmux session management for subagent tasks. Each OpenCode session gets a dedicated tmux session, and each subagent task gets its own window.

enabled.description: Enable tmux session management. Requires tmux to be installed.

sessionPrefix.description: Prefix for tmux session names. Sessions are named {prefix}-{opencode-session-id}.
